title: feuilleea subcoriacea
intro: Feuilleea subcoriacea, also known as the Australian water lily or water arum, is a type of aquatic plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Feuilleea subcoriacea include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as providing relief from fever and pain. The plant can be prepared for herbal use by making a tea from its leaves, roots, or rhizomes, or by using it in a topical cream or ointment.
